session.user: Run the fast CI checks: make ci-fast 2>&1 | tail -40
session.assistant: I'll run the fast CI checks for you.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run fast CI checks and show last 40 lines

session.tool_result: make: *** No rule to make target 'ci-fast'. Stop.
session.assistant: Let me check what CI targets are available in the Makefile:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: List available make targets related to CI

session.assistant: Let me check the Makefile directly:
session.tool_result:
1→.PHONY: format-check lint prettier prettier-check ty upgrade-ty test py-fast-ci fast-ci all-ci md-check docs-check docs-validate docs-sync-check docs-fix clean publish fix reinstall-erk-tools docs docs-serve docs-deploy exec-reference-check mcp mcp-dev test-erk-mcp docs-v2-build docs-v2-serve 2→ 3→prettier: 4→ prettier --write '**/*.md' --ignore-path .gitignore 5→ 6→prettier-check: 7→ prettier --check '**/*.md' --ignore-path .gitignore 8→ 9→format-check: 10→ uv run ruff format --check 11→ 12→lint: 13→ uv run ruff check 14→ 15→fix: 16→ uv run ruff check --fix --unsafe-fixes 17→ uv run ruff format 18→ prettier --write '**/*.md' --ignore-path .gitignore 19→ 20→ty: 21→ uv run ty check 22→ 23→upgrade-ty: 24→ uv remove ty --group dev && uv add --dev ty 25→ 26→# === Package-specific test targets === 27→ 28→test-erk-dev: 29→ cd packages/erk-dev && uv run pytest -n auto 30→
... [165 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Now I'll run the `fast-ci` target (which is the correct target name):

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run fast CI checks and show last 40 lines

session.assistant: The fast CI checks show that the erk-mcp tests failed. Let me get more detail on what failed:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run erk-mcp tests with verbose output

session.tool_result:
============================= test session starts ============================== platform linux -- Python 3.13.12, pytest-9.0.2, pluggy-1.6.0 rootdir: /home/runner/work/erk/erk configfile: pyproject.toml plugins: anyio-4.12.1, cov-7.0.0, xdist-3.8.0, asyncio-1.3.0 asyncio: mode=Mode.AUTO, debug=False, asyncio_default_fixture_loop_scope=function, asyncio_default_test_loop_scope=function collected 24 items
tests/test_main.py ........... [ 45%] tests/test_server.py ............. [100%]
============================== 24 passed in 0.82s ==============================
session.assistant: Good! The erk-mcp tests passed when run individually. Let me run the complete fast-ci check again to see the full output and identify where the issue occurs:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run complete fast CI checks with full output

session.tool_result:
Exit code 2 === Fast CI ===
--- Lint --- All checks passed!
--- Format Check --- 1662 files already formatted
--- Prettier Check --- Checking formatting... [[33mwarn[39m] docs/learned/integrations/github-review-decision.md [[33mwarn[39m] docs/learned/tui/status-indicators.md [[33mwarn[39m] Code style issues found in 2 files. Run Prettier with --write to fix.
--- Markdown Check --- ✓ AGENTS.md standard: PASSED
All CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md files properly paired.
CLAUDE.md files checked: 19 Violations: 0
--- Exec Reference Check --- OK: .claude/skills/erk-exec/reference.md is up to date
--- ty --- All checks passed!
--- Unit Tests (erk) ---
... [106 lines omitted] ...
make: *** [Makefile:97: fast-ci] Error 1
session.assistant:
## Fast CI Results
The fast CI checks have identified the following issues:
**FAILURES:**
1. **Prettier formatting issues** (2 files) - `docs/learned/integrations/github-review-decision.md` - `docs/learned/tui/status-indicators.md`
**SUCCESSES:**
- Lint: All checks passed - Format check: All files properly formatted (1662 files) - Markdown check: All AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md paired correctly - Exec reference check: OK - Type checking (ty): All checks passed - Unit tests (erk): 5875 passed - Tests (erk-dev): 136 passed - Tests (erk-statusline): 115 passed - Tests (erk-mcp): 24 passed
**Summary:** The fast CI is failing due to Prettier not formatting 2 markdown documentation files. All tests pass successfully. The issue is that the Prettier check step is finding code style violations in the markdown files.
